description: Letter from a woman in Fustat to her sons. In a mixture of Judaeo-Arabic and Arabic written in an elegant scribal hand. The writer reports the dire conditions in Fustat, including the plunder of the Dār al-Fā'izī (in the Judaeo-Arabic portion) and conveys news of the sick children in the house who are worsening day by day (in the Arabic portion). On verso are jottings in Arabic script, unclear if connected to recto. (Information from Goitein's index cards and Renee Levine Melammed.)
tags: illness: children's, illness letter 969-1517, illness: marad, Arabic script, Illness, Women's letters
description_he: מכתב מאשה בפוסטאט לבניה. תערובת של יהודית-ערבית וערבית. הכותבת מדווחת על מצב קשה בפוסטאט, כולל ביזת דאר אל-פאיזי, ועל ילדים חולים שמצבם מחמיר מיום ליום.
